title: Smoky Chewy Kenyan Bread Stew
description: A hearty, onepot Kenyan stew that turns stale almondflour bread into chewy, smoky goodness, perfect for a paleo-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the diced carrots, cubed sweet potato, and smoked paprika; cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the diced tomatoes and vegetable stock, bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the cubed stale bread to the pot, ensuring it’s submerged in the liquid.
Cover and let the stew simmer on low heat for 20 minutes, or until the bread is chewy and the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the smoked sea salt and adjust seasoning to taste.
Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot.
Enjoy the smoky, chewy texture of the bread and the comforting flavors of Kenyan spices.
